items.name: Anubis
items.summary: Anubis — anti-bot software that adds proof-of-work friction for crawlers.
items.why_included: A practical anti-bot tool surfaced as a low-friction alternative for sites fighting scrapers.
items.evidence: One commenter said OpenWRT uses Anubis and preferred waiting over "finding Waldos"; another described it as the tool that uses proof-of-work instead.

items.name: CanvasBlocker
items.summary: CanvasBlocker — Firefox extension that alters JS APIs to blunt fingerprinting.
items.why_included: Commenters pointed to it as a working browser-side workaround against fingerprint-heavy pages.
items.evidence: A commenter said they had used it for a long time and that it got through the Turnstile page.

items.name: LibreWolf
items.summary: LibreWolf — privacy-focused Firefox fork with anti-fingerprinting defaults.
items.why_included: Readers suggested it as a clean way around the WebGL fingerprinting problem.
items.evidence: A commenter said they use LibreWolf with WebGL disabled by default and do not hit the issue.

items.name: Creapure
items.source_story_title: Creatine raises brain energy levels and slows cognitive decline: study
items.summary: Creapure — German creatine brand commenters trust for purity.
items.why_included: The thread turned into a concrete brand recommendation instead of generic supplement talk.
items.evidence: A commenter said they use Creapure and that it seems legit, while others called it the purity-focused option.

items.name: Signal
items.source_story_title: Meta launches Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp subscriptions
items.summary: Signal — encrypted messenger commenters said spreads once people start using it.
items.why_included: Commenters used Signal as the obvious alternative to Meta’s messaging stack.
items.evidence: One commenter said mentioning Signal led friends and family to adopt it too.

items.name: Odysseus
items.summary: Odysseus — self-hosted AI workspace built from mobile shells and on-device agents.
items.why_included: The project stood out for its mobile-first, on-device provenance rather than just another cloud AI shell.
items.evidence: The author said a chunk was built from a phone using Termux, PWA install, and on-device agents.

items.name: Atomic Editor
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Atomic Editor – Obsidian-style live preview for CodeMirror 6
items.summary: Atomic Editor — Obsidian-style live preview editor for CodeMirror 6.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as a serious open-source take on a hard editing UX.
items.evidence: One commenter called it a good, production-quality open-source take that gets a lot right.

items.name: Roto
items.source_story_title: One year of Roto, a compiled scripting language for Rust
items.summary: Roto — compiled scripting language for Rust aimed at replacing shell scripts.
items.why_included: The thread showed real enthusiasm from adjacent Rust and scripting users.
items.evidence: A commenter said they’d love to replace more POSIX scripts with something saner, and another said they would add support in Rama.

items.name: S.E.C.R.E.T
items.summary: S.E.C.R.E.T — archive of security-envelope pattern examples with a polished presentation.
items.why_included: The archive got unusually strong aesthetic praise, not just technical curiosity.
items.evidence: Commenters called it a beautiful website and a very cool site with great design.
